headline: Sandisk Spun Off From Western Digital Last Year. Now It’s a $200 Billion Company.
summary: Sandisk  shares rose once more Tuesday, with the memory-chip company joining the likes of  McDonald’s Verizon  and  PepsiCo  when it comes to market value.  Sandisk  stock at Tuesday’s closing bell surpassed a $200 billion market capitalization for the first time since the company completed its spinoff from  Western Digital  on Feb. 24, 2025, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  Sandisk advanced 12% to $1,406.32 on Tuesday, as investors continued betting that the boom in construction of artificial-intelligence data centers will drive up memory demand.

headline: 27% of Americans Own ETFs but Allocate Just 6%, Here’s Why That Costs Them Retirement Income
summary: The Charles Schwab Modern Wealth Survey 2025 found that 27% of American investors own ETFs, yet those funds account for only 6% of the average portfolio. Individual stocks: 25%; mutual funds: 13%; cryptocurrency: 10%. The mismatch matters most for pre-retirees, because dividend-focused ETFs package the income-producing equity asset class into a single low-cost holding that ... 27% of Americans Own ETFs but Allocate Just 6%, Here’s Why That Costs Them Retirement Income

headline: Verizon Extends 5G Frontline Access As Valuation Gap Draws Attention
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) has expanded its 5G Frontline Network Slice to support more compatible laptops, tablets, and smartphones. The update gives a wider range of first responders access to dedicated, secure, high-speed 5G connectivity during emergency operations. For investors tracking NYSE:VZ, this product update sits alongside a share price of $47.57 and a return of 17.4% year to date. The company is putting clear focus on public sector and mission critical use cases, which can...

headline: Verizon Has a Dividend Yield Of 5.9% and Nike's Is 3.7%. But Which High-Yielding Dividend Stock Is a Better Buy?
summary: Both companies offer attractive dividends and long histories of annual increases. But under the surface, the cash backing those payouts looks quite different.
